title: Report Plan Outcome Response
description: Response from reporting an action outcome. Only returned to the orchestrator (buyer-side agent) that manages the plan. Sellers report delivery data via check_governance with phase 'delivery', not via this task.

outcome_id.description: Unique identifier for this outcome record.

outcome_state.description: Outcome state. 'accepted' means state updated with no issues. 'findings' means issues were detected. Renamed from `status` in 3.1 to free the top-level `status` key for the envelope task-status (TaskStatus) under MCP flat-on-the-wire serialization.

committed_budget.description: Budget committed from this outcome. Present for 'completed' and 'failed' outcomes.

findings.description: Issues detected. Present only when outcome_state is 'findings'.

category_id.description: Which validation category flagged the issue.

plan_summary.description: Updated plan budget state. Present for 'completed' and 'failed' outcomes.

total_committed.description: Total budget committed across all campaigns in the plan.

replayed.description: Set to true when this response was returned from the idempotency cache rather than from a fresh execution. Set to false (or omitted) when the request was executed fresh. Buyers use this to distinguish cached replays from new executions — matters for billing reconciliation, audit logs, state-machine routing (cached state-tracking fields are historical snapshots, not current state — re-read via the resource's read endpoint), and any downstream system that assumes exactly-once event semantics. From 3.1 onward, `replayed` MAY appear on responses to any request that resolved via the idempotency cache, including read tools — universal `idempotency_key` (see security.mdx §Idempotency) means the cache holds read responses too.
